action.reasoning: Observation: previous failure was due to relative checkpoint path after chdir; workspace shows train.py still creates save_dir before chdir but not via absolute path, and predict.py still changes extra config fields beyond the two required overrides. Analysis: we need a robust end-to-end baseline now, with absolute path handling and strict Habitat config usage to avoid eval-time failures. Since heuristic control is cheap and likely stronger than short RL, keep it but make scripts contract-safe. Decision: patch train.py and predict.py to absolutize paths before any chdir, remove extra config mutations, add deterministic seeding via env.seed if available, run short train plus local 5-episode predict self-test, then submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --episodes 3 --save_dir checkpoints/baseline && mkdir -p local_eval && python predict.py --data_path "$(pwd)/local_eval" --checkpoint "$(pwd)/checkpoints/baseline" --output "$(pwd)/local_eval/out.json" --num_episodes 5
